newsSinceJun6: No material fundamental news since June 6. Flow/momentum-press coverage only: retail/momentum articles (Jun 8-9) revisiting the May 13 Q1 beat (rev $839.2M vs ~$823M est, Q2 guide $875-905M vs $857.8M Street) and the >20% AI data-center growth framing; routine $0.10 quarterly dividend (record Jun 18, payable Jun 29); GuruFocus flagged the stock as overvalued after the run. The 200A EV power-module launch was June 3, outside the window. Stock flat today (+0.09%) after the massive YTD rerating (+274.6%).

sources.label: Quiver Quantitative - VSH jumps as investors revisit Q1 results and raised outlook
GuruFocus - VSH up 11.6% but GF Value says overvalued
TradingView/Zacks - Can AI demand become a meaningful growth engine for Vishay?

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already moved VSH down for the +274% YTD rerating; today brought no new fundamentals and a flat tape (+0.09%), so the crowding penalty is priced into the rank. Momentum-chaser coverage (Zacks, GuruFocus, StocksToTrade) keeps eroding the 'underfollowed' leg, hence small negative drift.
verdicts.bull: Tantalum/polymer/film/inductor exposure to AI server PDNs is genuinely bottleneck-critical, AI data-center revenue is guided to grow >20% off a sub-$100M base, and Itzehoe 12-inch capex adds real capacity into the cycle.
verdicts.neutral: Thesis intact but fully discovered: Q1 beat and raised Q2 guide are known, and at $8.8B cap with heavy retail-momentum coverage the name sits between hidden alpha and consensus AI-passives play.
verdicts.bear: A +274% YTD move on a low-margin cyclical (GAAP EPS $0.05 in Q1) leaves little residual alpha; any passives inventory wobble or AI-capex digestion triggers a sharp de-rating from a crowded base.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 print (Aug) confirming the $875-905M guide and AI data-center revenue ramping >20%.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Momentum unwind in re-rated AI-passives names; valuation now embeds flawless execution from a historically cyclical business.

verdicts.rankRationale: Network-centrality lens: Vishay's position in AI power chains (passives, discretes, current sense) is structurally unchanged and no new node-level information emerged since June 6; yesterday's down move already reconciled rank with the rerating, so hold.
verdicts.bull: Every AI power tree still runs through mundane passives and discretes; Vishay's breadth plus the Itzehoe 12-inch fab buildout deepens its centrality as power density per rack climbs.
verdicts.neutral: Centrality is real but commoditized at the edges; AI is still a small (sub-10%) slice of revenue, so the node matters more to the network map than the network does to Vishay's P&L yet.
verdicts.bear: The 'latent' label no longer fits a +274% YTD name with momentum coverage; if AI revenue stays a rounding error versus auto/industrial cyclicality, the node premium fades.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Evidence of AI data-center content per rack rising (power infrastructure and optical comms design wins flagged on Q1 call).
verdicts.primaryRisk: Auto/industrial end-market cyclicality swamping the AI signal; capacity adds hitting just as passives pricing normalizes.

verdicts.rankRationale: Broad audit page already sits VSH at a measured rank 90 after yesterday's down move for the YTD rerating; with no fresh fundamentals and a flat day, there is nothing to re-litigate.
verdicts.bull: Board-level power and passives content scales with AI server unit growth; Q1 beat, raised Q2 guide, and $400-440M capex plan show management leaning into the cycle.
verdicts.neutral: Real but indirect AI exposure inside a diversified cyclical; the score correctly balances content gains against market-cap recognition already received.
verdicts.bear: After a ~4x YTD move, the alpha left at rank 90 is mostly mean-reversion risk; thin GAAP profitability gives little valuation support if the AI-passives narrative cools.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 2026 results validating the raised revenue guide and AI-related revenue trajectory.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Post-rerating crowding unwind; passives pricing/inventory cycle turning before AI revenue is large enough to matter.
